items.question: The linear regression hypothesis for one example is:
items.explanation: Linear regression outputs a real-valued linear combination of features plus bias.

items.question: OLS training minimizes which objective?
items.explanation: Ordinary least squares minimizes average squared residual (y − ŷ)² on training data.

items.question: The closed-form OLS solution (full rank X) is:
items.explanation: Derived from setting the MSE gradient to zero — the normal equation.

items.question: Geometrically, OLS predictions ŷ are:
options: The projection of y onto the column space of X
The projection of X onto y
items.answer: The projection of y onto the column space of X
items.explanation: Least squares finds the closest point in span(X columns) to target vector y.

items.question: Why include a column of ones in X?
items.explanation: The weight on the constant feature acts as the intercept term.

items.question: Minimizing MSE on y = Xw + ε with Gaussian ε is equivalent to:
items.explanation: Gaussian noise makes the log-likelihood proportional to negative squared error.
